mapping.a_element: non-Abelian anyons as quasiparticle excitations with non-Abelian braiding statistics
mapping.b_element: non-Abelian anyons as fundamental entities in anyon models with braiding properties

mapping.a_element: topological degeneracy of multi-quasiparticle states storing quantum information
mapping.b_element: superpositions of total anyonic charge in a target region as measurable quantum states

mapping.a_element: braiding quasiparticles to perform unitary gate operations for quantum computation
mapping.b_element: monodromy of probe anyons in interferometry collapsing superpositions of anyonic charge

mapping.a_element: fault-tolerance from non-local encoding of quasiparticle states
mapping.b_element: distinguishability of anyonic charges via interferometric measurement outcomes

mapping.a_element: ν=5/2 fractional quantum Hall state as a non-Abelian topological state
mapping.b_element: ν=5/2 Moore-Read state as a specific anyon model for interferometry experiments

brief_justification: Both papers center on the same underlying machinery: non-Abelian anyons with braiding statistics as the core mechanism. Paper A uses braiding for fault-tolerant quantum computation, while Paper B uses interferometry to measure anyonic charge via monodromy, but both rely on the non-Abelian braiding properties and topological degeneracy of anyonic states described in the abstracts.
